2.strong: I will give to drink/ you

5.strong: she was in labor with/ you

krv.SNG_1_2: 내게 입맞추기를 원하니 네 사랑이 포도주보다 나음이로구나
krv.SNG_1_3: 네 기름이 향기로워 아름답고 네 이름이 쏟은 향기름 같으므로 처녀들이 너를 사랑하는구나
krv.SNG_1_4: 왕이 나를 침궁으로 이끌어 들이시니 너는 나를 인도하라 우리가 너를 따라 달려가리라 우리가 너를 인하여 기뻐하며 즐거워하니 네 사랑이 포도주에서 지남이라 처녀들이 너를 사랑함이 마땅하니라
krv.SNG_1_5: 예루살렘 여자들아 내가 비록 검으나 아름다우니 게달의 장막 같을지라도 솔로몬의 휘장과도 같구나
krv.SNG_1_6: 내가 일광에 쬐어서 거무스름할지라도 흘겨보지 말 것은 내 어미의 아들들이 나를 노하여 포도원지기를 삼았음이라 나의 포도원은 내가 지키지 못하였구나
krv.SNG_1_7: 내 마음에 사랑하는 자야 너의 양떼 먹이는 곳과 오정에 쉬게 하는 곳을 내게 고하라 내가 네 동무 양떼 곁에서 어찌 얼굴을 가리운 자 같이 되랴
krv.SNG_1_8: 여인 중에 어여쁜 자야 네가 알지 못하겠거든 양떼의 발자취를 따라 목자들의 장막 곁에서 너의 염소 새끼를 먹일지니라
krv.SNG_1_9: 내 사랑아 내가 너를 바로의 병거의 준마에 비하였구나
krv.SNG_1_10: 네 두 뺨은 땋은 머리털로, 네 목은 구슬 꿰미로 아름답구나
krv.SNG_1_11: 우리가 너를 위하여 금사슬을 은을 박아 만들리라
krv.SNG_1_12: 왕이 상에 앉았을 때에 나의 나도 기름이 향기를 토하였구나
krv.SNG_1_13: 나의 사랑하는 자는 내 품 가운데 몰약 향낭이요
krv.SNG_1_14: 나의 사랑하는 자는 내게 엔게디 포도원의 고벨화 송이로구나
krv.SNG_1_15: 내 사랑아 너는 어여쁘고 어여쁘다 네 눈이 비둘기 같구나
krv.SNG_1_16: 나의 사랑하는 자야 너는 어여쁘고 화창하다 우리의 침상은 푸르고
krv.SNG_1_17: 우리 집은 백향목 들보, 잣나무 석가래로구나
krv.SNG_2_2: 여자들 중에 내 사랑은 가시나무 가운데 백합화 같구나
krv.SNG_2_3: 남자들 중에 나의 사랑하는 자는 수풀 가운데 사과나무 같구나 내가 그 그늘에 앉아서 심히 기뻐하였고 그 실과는 내 입에 달았구나
krv.SNG_2_4: 그가 나를 인도하여 잔치집에 들어갔으니 그 사랑이 내 위에 기로구나
krv.SNG_2_5: 너희는 건포도로 내 힘을 돕고 사과로 나를 시원케 하라 내가 사랑하므로 병이 났음이니라
krv.SNG_2_6: 그가 왼손으로 내 머리에 베게하고 오른손으로 나를 안는구나
krv.SNG_2_7: 예루살렘 여자들아 내가 노루와 들사슴으로 너희에게 부탁한다 내 사랑이 원하기 전에는 흔들지 말고 깨우지 말지니라
krv.SNG_2_8: 나의 사랑하는 자의 목소리로구나 보라 그가 산에서 달리고 작은 산을 빨리 넘어 오는구나
krv.SNG_2_9: 나의 사랑하는 자는 노루와도 같고 어린 사슴과도 같아서 우리 벽 뒤에 서서 창으로 들여다 보며 창살 틈으로 엿보는구나
krv.SNG_2_10: 나의 사랑하는 자가 내게 말하여 이르기를 나의 사랑 나의 어여쁜 자야 일어나서 함께 가자
krv.SNG_2_12: 지면에는 꽃이 피고 새의 노래할 때가 이르렀는데 반구의 소리가 우리 땅에 들리는구나
krv.SNG_2_13: 무화과 나무에는 푸른 열매가 익었고 포도나무는 꽃이 피어 향기를 토하는구나 나의 사랑 나의 어여쁜 자야 일어나서 함께 가자
krv.SNG_2_14: 바위 틈 낭떠러지 은밀한 속에 있는 나의 비둘기야 나로 네 얼굴을 보게 하라 네 소리를 듣게 하라 네 소리는 부드럽고 네 얼굴은 아름답구나
krv.SNG_2_15: 우리를 위하여 여우 곧 포도원을 허는 작은 여우를 잡으라 우리의 포도원에 꽃이 피었음이니라
krv.SNG_2_16: 나의 사랑하는 자는 내게 속하였고 나는 그에게 속하였구나 그가 백합화 가운데서 양떼를 먹이는구나
krv.SNG_2_17: 나의 사랑하는 자야 날이 기울고 그림자가 갈 때에 돌아와서 베데르 산에서의 노루와 어린 사슴 같아여라
krv.SNG_3_1: 내가 밤에 침상에서 마음에 사랑하는 자를 찾았구나 찾아도 발견치 못하였구나
krv.SNG_3_2: 이에 내가 일어나서 성중으로 돌아다니며 마음에 사랑하는 자를 거리에서나 큰 길에서나 찾으리라 하고 찾으나 만나지 못하였구나
krv.SNG_3_3: 성중의 행순하는 자들을 만나서 묻기를 내 마음에 사랑하는 자를 너희가 보았느냐 하고
krv.SNG_3_4: 그들을 떠나자마자 마음에 사랑하는 자를 만나서 그를 붙잡고 내 어미 집으로, 나를 잉태한 자의 방으로 가기까지 놓지 아니하였노라
krv.SNG_3_5: 예루살렘 여자들아 내가 노루와 들 사슴으로 너희에게 부탁한다 사랑하는 자가 원하기 전에는 흔들지 말고 깨우지 말지니라
krv.SNG_3_6: 연기 기둥과도 같고 몰약과 유향과 장사의 여러가지 향품으로 향기롭게도 하고 거친 들에서 오는 자가 누구인고
krv.SNG_3_7: 이는 솔로몬의 연이라 이스라엘 용사 중 육십 인이 옹위하였는데
krv.SNG_3_8: 다 칼을 잡고 싸움에 익숙한 사람들이라 밤의 두려움을 인하여 각기 허리에 칼을 찼느니라
krv.SNG_3_9: 솔로몬 왕이 레바논 나무로 자기의 연을 만들었는데
krv.SNG_3_10: 그 기둥은 은이요 바닥은 금이요 자리는 자색 담이라 그 안에는 예루살렘 여자들의 사랑이 입혔구나
krv.SNG_3_11: 시온의 여자들아 나와서 솔로몬 왕을 보라 혼인날 마음이 기쁠 때에 그 모친의 씌운 면류관이 그 머리에 있구나
krv.SNG_4_1: 내 사랑 너는 어여쁘고도 어여쁘다 너울 속에 있는 네 눈이 비둘기 같고 네 머리털은 길르앗 산 기슭에 누운 무리 염소 같구나
krv.SNG_4_2: 네 이는 목욕장에서 나온 털 깎인 암양 곧 새끼 없는 것은 하나도 없이 각각 쌍태를 낳은 양 같구나
krv.SNG_4_3: 네 입술은 홍색 실 같고 네 입은 어여쁘고 너울 속의 네 뺨은 석류 한 쪽 같구나
krv.SNG_4_4: 네 목은 군기를 두려고 건축한 다윗의 망대 곧 일천 방패, 용사의 모든 방패가 달린 망대 같고
krv.SNG_4_5: 네 두 유방은 백합화 가운데서 꼴을 먹는 쌍태 노루 새끼 같구나
krv.SNG_4_6: 날이 기울고 그림자가 갈 때에 내가 몰약 산과 유향의 작은 산으로 가리라
krv.SNG_4_7: 나의 사랑 너는 순전히 어여뻐서 아무 흠이 없구나
krv.SNG_4_8: 나의 신부야 너는 레바논에서부터 나와 함께 하고 레바논에서부터 나와 함께 가자 아마나와 스닐과 헤르몬 꼭대기에서 사자 굴과 표범 산에서 내려다보아라
krv.SNG_4_9: 나의 누이, 나의 신부야 네가 내 마음을 빼앗았구나 네 눈으로 한번 보는 것과 네 목의 구슬 한 꿰미로 내 마음을 빼앗았구나
krv.SNG_4_10: 나의 누이, 나의 신부야 네 사랑이 어찌 그리 아름다운지 네 사랑은 포도주에 지나고 네 기름의 향기는 각양 향품보다 승하구나
krv.SNG_4_11: 내 신부야 네 입술에서는 꿀 방울이 떨어지고 네 혀 밑에는 꿀과 젖이 있고 네 의복의 향기는 레바논의 향기 같구나
krv.SNG_4_12: 나의 누이, 나의 신부는 잠근 동산이요 덮은 우물이요 봉한 샘이로구나
krv.SNG_4_13: 네게서 나는 것은 석류나무와 각종 아름다운 과수와 고벨화와 나도초와
krv.SNG_4_14: 나도와 번홍화와 창포와 계수와 각종 유향목과 몰약과 침향과 모든 귀한 향품이요
krv.SNG_4_15: 너는 동산의 샘이요 생수의 우물이요 레바논에서부터 흐르는 시내로구나
krv.SNG_4_16: 북풍아 일어나라 남풍아 오라 나의 동산에 불어서 향기를 날리라 나의 사랑하는 자가 그 동산에 들어가서 그 아름다운 실과 먹기를 원하노라
krv.SNG_5_1: 나의 누이, 나의 신부야 내가 내 동산에 들어와서 나의 몰약과 향재료를 거두고 나의 꿀송이와 꿀을 먹고 내 포도주와 내 젖을 마셨으니 나의 친구들아 먹으라 나의 사랑하는 사람들아 마시고 많이 마시라
krv.SNG_5_2: 내가 잘지라도 마음은 깨었는데 나의 사랑하는 자의 소리가 들리는구나 문을 두드려 이르기를 나의 누이, 나의 사랑, 나의 비둘기, 나의 완전한 자야 문 열어 다고 내 머리에는 이슬이, 내 머리털에는 밤 이슬이 가득하였다 하는구나
krv.SNG_5_3: 내가 옷을 벗었으니 어찌 다시 입겠으며 내가 발을 씻었으니 어찌 다시 더럽히랴마는
krv.SNG_5_4: 나의 사랑하는 자가 문틈으로 손을 들이밀매 내 마음이 동하여서
krv.SNG_5_5: 일어나서 나의 사랑하는 자 위하여 문을 열 때 몰약이 내 손에서, 몰약의 즙이 내 손가락에서 문빗장에 듣는구나
krv.SNG_5_6: 내가 나의 사랑하는 자 위하여 문을 열었으나 그가 벌써 물러갔네 그가 말할 때에 내 혼이 나갔구나 내가 그를 찾아도 못 만났고 불러도 응답이 없었구나
krv.SNG_5_7: 성중에서 행순하는 자들이 나를 만나매 나를 쳐서 상하게 하였고 성벽을 파수하는 자들이 나의 웃옷을 벗겨 취하였구나
krv.SNG_5_8: 예루살렘 여자들아 너희에게 내가 부탁한다 너희가 나의 사랑하는 자를 만나거든 내가 사랑하므로 병이 났다고 하려무나
krv.SNG_5_9: 여자 중 극히 어여쁜 자야 너의 사랑하는 자가 남의 사랑하는 자보다 나은 것이 무엇인가 너의 사랑하는 자가 남의 사랑하는 자보다 나은 것이 무엇이기에 이같이 우리에게 부탁하는가
krv.SNG_5_10: 나의 사랑하는 자는 희고도 붉어 만 사람에 뛰어난다
krv.SNG_5_11: 머리는 정금 같고 머리털은 고불고불하고 까마귀 같이 검구나
krv.SNG_5_12: 눈은 시냇가의 비둘기 같은데 젖으로 씻은 듯하고 아름답게도 박혔구나
krv.SNG_5_13: 뺨은 향기로운 꽃밭 같고 향기로운 풀언덕과도 같고 입술은 백합화 같고 몰약의 즙이 뚝뚝 떨어진다
krv.SNG_5_14: 손은 황옥을 물린 황금 노리개 같고 몸은 아로새긴 상아에 청옥을 입힌듯 하구나
krv.SNG_5_15: 다리는 정금 받침에 세운 화반석 기둥 같고 형상은 레바논 같고 백향목처럼 보기 좋고
krv.SNG_5_16: 입은 심히 다니 그 전체가 사랑스럽구나 예루살렘 여자들아 이는 나의 사랑하는 자요 나의 친구일다
krv.SNG_6_1: 여자 중 극히 어여쁜 자야 너의 사랑하는 자가 어디로 갔는가 너의 사랑하는 자가 어디로 돌이켰는가 우리가 너와 함께 찾으리라
krv.SNG_6_2: 나의 사랑하는 자가 자기 동산으로 내려가 향기로운 꽃밭에 이르러서 동산 가운데서 양떼를 먹이며 백합화를 꺾는구나
krv.SNG_6_3: 나는 나의 사랑하는 자에게 속하였고 나의 사랑하는 자는 내게 속하였다 그가 백합화 가운데서 그 양떼를 먹이는구나
krv.SNG_6_4: 내 사랑아 너의 어여쁨이 디르사 같고 너의 고움이 예루살렘 같고 엄위함이 기치를 벌인 군대 같구나
krv.SNG_6_5: 네 눈이 나를 놀래니 돌이켜 나를 보지 말라 네 머리털은 길르앗 산 기슭에 누운 염소떼 같고
krv.SNG_6_6: 네 이는 목욕장에서 나온 암양떼 곧 새끼 없는 것은 하나도 없이 각각 쌍태를 낳은 양 같고
krv.SNG_6_7: 너울 속의 너의 뺨은 석류 한 쪽 같구나
krv.SNG_6_8: 왕후가 육십이요 비빈이 팔십이요 시녀가 무수하되
krv.SNG_6_9: 나의 비둘기, 나의 완전한 자는 하나뿐이로구나 그는 그 어미의 외딸이요 그 낳은 자의 귀중히 여기는 자로구나 여자들이 그를 보고 복된 자라 하고 왕후와 비빈들도 그를 칭찬하는구나
krv.SNG_6_10: 아침빛 같이 뚜렷하고 달 같이 아름답고 해 같이 맑고 기치를 벌인 군대 같이 엄위한 여자가 누구인가
krv.SNG_6_11: 골짜기의 푸른 초목을 보려고 포도나무가 순이 났는가 석류나무가 꽃이 피었는가 알려고 내가 호도 동산으로 내려갔을 때에
krv.SNG_6_12: 부지 중에 내 마음이 나로 내 귀한 백성의 수레 가운데 이르게 하였구나
krv.SNG_6_13: 돌아오고 돌아오라 술람미 여자야 돌아오고 돌아오라 우리로 너를 보게 하라
krv.SNG_6_14: 너희가 어찌하여 마하나임의 춤추는 것을 보는 것처럼 술람미 여자를 보려느냐
krv.SNG_7_1: 귀한 자의 딸아 신을 신은 네 발이 어찌 그리 아름다운가 네 넓적다리는 둥글어서 공교한 장색의 만든 구슬 꿰미 같구나
krv.SNG_7_2: 배꼽은 섞은 포도주를 가득히 부은 둥근 잔 같고 허리는 백합화로 두른 밀단 같구나
krv.SNG_7_3: 두 유방은 암사슴의 쌍태 새끼 같고
krv.SNG_7_4: 목은 상아 망대 같구나 눈은 헤스본 바드랍빔 문 곁의 못 같고 코는 다메섹을 향한 레바논 망대 같구나
krv.SNG_7_5: 머리는 갈멜산 같고 드리운 머리털은 자주 빛이 있으니 왕이 그 머리카락에 매이었구나
krv.SNG_7_6: 사랑아 네가 어찌 그리 아름다운지, 어찌 그리 화창한지 쾌락하게 하는구나
krv.SNG_7_7: 네 키는 종려나무 같고 네 유방은 그 열매 송이 같구나
krv.SNG_7_8: 내가 말하기를 종려나무에 올라가서 그 가지를 잡으리라 하였나니 네 유방은 포도송이 같고 네 콧김은 사과 냄새 같고
krv.SNG_7_9: 네 입은 좋은 포도주 같을 것이니라 이 포도주는 나의 사랑하는 자를 위하여 미끄럽게 흘러 내려서 자는 자의 입으로 움직이게 하느니라
krv.SNG_7_10: 나는 나의 사랑하는 자에게 속하였구나 그가 나를 사모하는구나
krv.SNG_7_11: 나의 사랑하는 자야 우리가 함께 들로 가서 동네에서 유숙하자
krv.SNG_7_12: 우리가 일찍이 일어나서 포도원으로 가서 포도 움이 돋았는지 꽃술이 퍼졌는지 석류꽃이 피었는지 보자 거기서 내가 나의 사랑을 네게 주리라
krv.SNG_7_13: 합환채가 향기를 토하고 우리의 문 앞에는 각양 귀한 실과가 새 것, 묵은 것이 구비하였구나 내가 나의 사랑하는 자 너를 위하여 쌓아둔 것이로구나
krv.SNG_8_1: 네가 내 어미의 젖을 먹은 오라비 같았었더면 내가 밖에서 너를 만날 때에 입을 맞추어도 나를 업신여길 자가 없었을 것이라
krv.SNG_8_2: 내가 너를 이끌어 내 어미 집에 들이고 네게서 교훈을 받았으리라 나는 향기로운 술 곧 석류즙으로 네게 마시웠겠고
krv.SNG_8_3: 너는 왼손으론 내 머리에 베개하고 오른손으론 나를 안았었으리라
krv.SNG_8_4: 예루살렘 여자들아 내가 너희에게 부탁한다 나의 사랑하는 자가 원하기 전에는 흔들지 말며 깨우지 말지니라
krv.SNG_8_5: 그 사랑하는 자를 의지하고 거친 들에서 올라오는 여자가 누구인고 너를 인하여 네 어미가 신고한, 너를 낳은 자가 애쓴 그곳 사과나무 아래서 내가 너를 깨웠노라
krv.SNG_8_6: 너는 나를 인 같이 마음에 품고 도장 같이 팔에 두라 사랑은 죽음 같이 강하고 투기는 음부 같이 잔혹하며 불 같이 일어나니 그 기세가 여호와의 불과 같으니라
krv.SNG_8_7: 이 사랑은 많은 물이 꺼치지 못하겠고 홍수라도 엄몰하지 못하나니 사람이 그 온 가산을 다 주고 사랑과 바꾸려 할지라도 오히려 멸시를 받으리라
krv.SNG_8_8: 우리에게 있는 작은 누이는 아직도 유방이 없구나 그가 청혼함을 받는 날에는 우리가 그를 위하여 무엇을 할꼬
krv.SNG_8_9: 그가 성벽일진대 우리는 은 망대를 그 위에 세울 것이요 그가 문일진대 우리는 백향목 판자로 두르리라
krv.SNG_8_10: 나는 성벽이요 나의 유방은 망대 같으니 그러므로 나는 그의 보기에 화평을 얻은 자 같구나
krv.SNG_8_11: 솔로몬이 바알하몬에 포도원이 있어 지키는 자들에게 맡겨두고 그들로 각기 그 실과를 인하여서 은 일천을 바치게 하였구나
krv.SNG_8_12: 솔로몬 너는 일천을 얻겠고 실과 지키는 자도 이백을 얻으려니와 내게 속한 내 포도원은 내 앞에 있구나
krv.SNG_8_13: 너 동산에 거한 자야 동무들이 네 소리에 귀를 기울이니 나로 듣게 하려무나
krv.SNG_8_14: 나의 사랑하는 자야 너는 빨리 달리라 향기로운 산들에서 노루와도 같고 어린 사슴과도 같아여라

kjv.SNG_1_1: The song of songs, which is Solomon's.
kjv.SNG_1_2: Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth: for thy love is better than wine.
kjv.SNG_1_3: Because of the savour of thy good ointments thy name is as ointment poured forth, therefore do the virgins love thee.
kjv.SNG_1_4: Draw me, we will run after thee: the king hath brought me into his chambers: we will be glad and rejoice in thee, we will remember thy love more than wine: the upright love thee.
kjv.SNG_1_5: I am black, but comely, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, as the tents of Kedar, as the curtains of Solomon.
kjv.SNG_1_6: Look not upon me, because I am black, because the sun hath looked upon me: my mother's children were angry with me; they made me the keeper of the vineyards; but mine own vineyard have I not kept.
kjv.SNG_1_7: Tell me, O thou whom my soul loveth, where thou feedest, where thou makest thy flock to rest at noon: for why should I be as one that turneth aside by the flocks of thy companions?
kjv.SNG_1_8: If thou know not, O thou fairest among women, go thy way forth by the footsteps of the flock, and feed thy kids beside the shepherds' tents.
kjv.SNG_1_9: I have compared thee, O my love, to a company of horses in Pharaoh's chariots.
kjv.SNG_1_10: Thy cheeks are comely with rows of jewels, thy neck with chains of gold.
kjv.SNG_1_11: We will make thee borders of gold with studs of silver.
kjv.SNG_1_12: While the king sitteth at his table, my spikenard sendeth forth the smell thereof.
kjv.SNG_1_13: A bundle of myrrh is my well-beloved unto me; he shall lie all night betwixt my breasts.
kjv.SNG_1_14: My beloved is unto me as a cluster of camphire in the vineyards of Engedi.
kjv.SNG_1_15: Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; thou hast doves' eyes.
kjv.SNG_1_16: Behold, thou art fair, my beloved, yea, pleasant: also our bed is green.
kjv.SNG_1_17: The beams of our house are cedar, and our rafters of fir.
kjv.SNG_2_1: I am the rose of Sharon, and the lily of the valleys.
kjv.SNG_2_2: As the lily among thorns, so is my love among the daughters.
kjv.SNG_2_3: As the apple tree among the trees of the wood, so is my beloved among the sons. I sat down under his shadow with great delight, and his fruit was sweet to my taste.
kjv.SNG_2_4: He brought me to the banqueting house, and his banner over me was love.
kjv.SNG_2_5: Stay me with flagons, comfort me with apples: for I am sick of love.
kjv.SNG_2_6: His left hand is under my head, and his right hand doth embrace me.
kjv.SNG_2_7: I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, till he please.
kjv.SNG_2_8: The voice of my beloved! behold, he cometh leaping upon the mountains, skipping upon the hills.
kjv.SNG_2_9: My beloved is like a roe or a young hart: behold, he standeth behind our wall, he looketh forth at the windows, shewing himself through the lattice.
kjv.SNG_2_10: My beloved spake, and said unto me, Rise up, my love, my fair one, and come away.
kjv.SNG_2_11: For, lo, the winter is past, the rain is over and gone;
kjv.SNG_2_12: The flowers appear on the earth; the time of the singing of birds is come, and the voice of the turtle is heard in our land;
kjv.SNG_2_13: The fig tree putteth forth her green figs, and the vines with the tender grape give a good smell. Arise, my love, my fair one, and come away.
kjv.SNG_2_14: O my dove, that art in the clefts of the rock, in the secret places of the stairs, let me see thy countenance, let me hear thy voice; for sweet is thy voice, and thy countenance is comely.
kjv.SNG_2_15: Take us the foxes, the little foxes, that spoil the vines: for our vines have tender grapes.
kjv.SNG_2_16: My beloved is mine, and I am his: he feedeth among the lilies.
kjv.SNG_2_17: Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, turn, my beloved, and be thou like a roe or a young hart upon the mountains of Bether.
kjv.SNG_3_1: By night on my bed I sought him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.
kjv.SNG_3_2: I will rise now, and go about the city in the streets, and in the broad ways I will seek him whom my soul loveth: I sought him, but I found him not.
kjv.SNG_3_3: The watchmen that go about the city found me: to whom I said, Saw ye him whom my soul loveth?
kjv.SNG_3_4: It was but a little that I passed from them, but I found him whom my soul loveth: I held him, and would not let him go, until I had brought him into my mother's house, and into the chamber of her that conceived me.
kjv.SNG_3_5: I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, till he please.
kjv.SNG_3_6: Who is this that cometh out of the wilderness like pillars of smoke, perfumed with myrrh and frankincense, with all powders of the merchant?
kjv.SNG_3_7: Behold his bed, which is Solomon's; threescore valiant men are about it, of the valiant of Israel.
kjv.SNG_3_8: They all hold swords, being expert in war: every man hath his sword upon his thigh because of fear in the night.
kjv.SNG_3_9: King Solomon made himself a chariot of the wood of Lebanon.
kjv.SNG_3_10: He made the pillars thereof of silver, the bottom thereof of gold, the covering of it of purple, the midst thereof being paved with love, for the daughters of Jerusalem.
kjv.SNG_3_11: Go forth, O ye daughters of Zion, and behold king Solomon with the crown wherewith his mother crowned him in the day of his espousals, and in the day of the gladness of his heart.
kjv.SNG_4_1: Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; thou hast doves' eyes within thy locks: thy hair is as a flock of goats, that appear from mount Gilead.
kjv.SNG_4_2: Thy teeth are like a flock of sheep that are even shorn, which came up from the washing; whereof every one bear twins, and none is barren among them.
kjv.SNG_4_3: Thy lips are like a thread of scarlet, and thy speech is comely: thy temples are like a piece of a pomegranate within thy locks.
kjv.SNG_4_4: Thy neck is like the tower of David builded for an armoury, whereon there hang a thousand bucklers, all shields of mighty men.
kjv.SNG_4_5: Thy two breasts are like two young roes that are twins, which feed among the lilies.
kjv.SNG_4_6: Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, I will get me to the mountain of myrrh, and to the hill of frankincense.
kjv.SNG_4_7: Thou art all fair, my love; there is no spot in thee.
kjv.SNG_4_8: Come with me from Lebanon, my spouse, with me from Lebanon: look from the top of Amana, from the top of Shenir and Hermon, from the lions' dens, from the mountains of the leopards.
kjv.SNG_4_9: Thou hast ravished my heart, my sister, my spouse; thou hast ravished my heart with one of thine eyes, with one chain of thy neck.
kjv.SNG_4_10: How fair is thy love, my sister, my spouse! how much better is thy love than wine! and the smell of thine ointments than all spices!
kjv.SNG_4_11: Thy lips, O my spouse, drop as the honeycomb: honey and milk are under thy tongue; and the smell of thy garments is like the smell of Lebanon.
kjv.SNG_4_12: A garden inclosed is my sister, my spouse; a spring shut up, a fountain sealed.
kjv.SNG_4_13: Thy plants are an orchard of pomegranates, with pleasant fruits; camphire, with spikenard,
kjv.SNG_4_14: Spikenard and saffron; calamus and cinnamon, with all trees of frankincense; myrrh and aloes, with all the chief spices:
kjv.SNG_4_15: A fountain of gardens, a well of living waters, and streams from Lebanon.
kjv.SNG_4_16: Awake, O north wind; and come, thou south; blow upon my garden, that the spices thereof may flow out. Let my beloved come into his garden, and eat his pleasant fruits.
kjv.SNG_5_1: I am come into my garden, my sister, my spouse: I have gathered my myrrh with my spice; I have eaten my honeycomb with my honey; I have drunk my wine with my milk: eat, O friends; drink, yea, drink abundantly, O beloved.
kjv.SNG_5_2: I sleep, but my heart waketh: it is the voice of my beloved that knocketh, saying, Open to me, my sister, my love, my dove, my undefiled: for my head is filled with dew, and my locks with the drops of the night.
kjv.SNG_5_3: I have put off my coat; how shall I put it on? I have washed my feet; how shall I defile them?
kjv.SNG_5_4: My beloved put in his hand by the hole of the door, and my bowels were moved for him.
kjv.SNG_5_5: I rose up to open to my beloved; and my hands dropped with myrrh, and my fingers with sweet smelling myrrh, upon the handles of the lock.
kjv.SNG_5_6: I opened to my beloved; but my beloved had withdrawn himself, and was gone: my soul failed when he spake: I sought him, but I could not find him; I called him, but he gave me no answer.
kjv.SNG_5_7: The watchmen that went about the city found me, they smote me, they wounded me; the keepers of the walls took away my veil from me.
kjv.SNG_5_8: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, if ye find my beloved, that ye tell him, that I am sick of love.
kjv.SNG_5_9: What is thy beloved more than another beloved, O thou fairest among women? what is thy beloved more than another beloved, that thou dost so charge us?
kjv.SNG_5_10: My beloved is white and ruddy, the chiefest among ten thousand.
kjv.SNG_5_11: His head is as the most fine gold, his locks are bushy, and black as a raven.
kjv.SNG_5_12: His eyes are as the eyes of doves by the rivers of waters, washed with milk, and fitly set.
kjv.SNG_5_13: His cheeks are as a bed of spices, as sweet flowers: his lips like lilies, dropping sweet smelling myrrh.
kjv.SNG_5_14: His hands are as gold rings set with the beryl: his belly is as bright ivory overlaid with sapphires.
kjv.SNG_5_15: His legs are as pillars of marble, set upon sockets of fine gold: his countenance is as Lebanon, excellent as the cedars.
kjv.SNG_5_16: His mouth is most sweet: yea, he is altogether lovely. This is my beloved, and this is my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem.
kjv.SNG_6_1: Whither is thy beloved gone, O thou fairest among women? whither is thy beloved turned aside? that we may seek him with thee.
kjv.SNG_6_2: My beloved is gone down into his garden, to the beds of spices, to feed in the gardens, and to gather lilies.
kjv.SNG_6_3: I am my beloved's, and my beloved is mine: he feedeth among the lilies.
kjv.SNG_6_4: Thou art beautiful, O my love, as Tirzah, comely as Jerusalem, terrible as an army with banners.
kjv.SNG_6_5: Turn away thine eyes from me, for they have overcome me: thy hair is as a flock of goats that appear from Gilead.
kjv.SNG_6_6: Thy teeth are as a flock of sheep which go up from the washing, whereof every one beareth twins, and there is not one barren among them.
kjv.SNG_6_7: As a piece of a pomegranate are thy temples within thy locks.
kjv.SNG_6_8: There are threescore queens, and fourscore concubines, and virgins without number.
kjv.SNG_6_9: My dove, my undefiled is but one; she is the only one of her mother, she is the choice one of her that bare her. The daughters saw her, and blessed her; yea, the queens and the concubines, and they praised her.
kjv.SNG_6_10: Who is she that looketh forth as the morning, fair as the moon, clear as the sun, and terrible as an army with banners?
kjv.SNG_6_11: I went down into the garden of nuts to see the fruits of the valley, and to see whether the vine flourished and the pomegranates budded.
kjv.SNG_6_12: Or ever I was aware, my soul made me like the chariots of Amminadib.
kjv.SNG_6_13: Return, return, O Shulamite; return, return, that we may look upon thee. What will ye see in the Shulamite? As it were the company of two armies.
kjv.SNG_7_1: How beautiful are thy feet with shoes, O prince's daughter! the joints of thy thighs are like jewels, the work of the hands of a cunning workman.
kjv.SNG_7_2: Thy navel is like a round goblet, which wanteth not liquor: thy belly is like an heap of wheat set about with lilies.
kjv.SNG_7_3: Thy two breasts are like two young roes that are twins.
kjv.SNG_7_4: Thy neck is as a tower of ivory; thine eyes like the fishpools in Heshbon, by the gate of Bathrabbim: thy nose is as the tower of Lebanon which looketh toward Damascus.
kjv.SNG_7_5: Thine head upon thee is like Carmel, and the hair of thine head like purple; the king is held in the galleries.
kjv.SNG_7_6: How fair and how pleasant art thou, O love, for delights!
kjv.SNG_7_7: This thy stature is like to a palm tree, and thy breasts to clusters of grapes.
kjv.SNG_7_8: I said, I will go up to the palm tree, I will take hold of the boughs thereof: now also thy breasts shall be as clusters of the vine, and the smell of thy nose like apples;
kjv.SNG_7_9: And the roof of thy mouth like the best wine for my beloved, that goeth down sweetly, causing the lips of those that are asleep to speak.
kjv.SNG_7_10: I am my beloved's, and his desire is toward me.
kjv.SNG_7_11: Come, my beloved, let us go forth into the field; let us lodge in the villages.
kjv.SNG_7_12: Let us get up early to the vineyards; let us see if the vine flourish, whether the tender grape appear, and the pomegranates bud forth: there will I give thee my loves.
kjv.SNG_7_13: The mandrakes give a smell, and at our gates are all manner of pleasant fruits, new and old, which I have laid up for thee, O my beloved.
kjv.SNG_8_1: O that thou wert as my brother, that sucked the breasts of my mother! when I should find thee without, I would kiss thee; yea, I should not be despised.
kjv.SNG_8_2: I would lead thee, and bring thee into my mother's house, who would instruct me: I would cause thee to drink of spiced wine of the juice of my pomegranate.
kjv.SNG_8_3: His left hand should be under my head, and his right hand should embrace me.
kjv.SNG_8_4: I charge you, O daughters of Jerusalem, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, until he please.
kjv.SNG_8_5: Who is this that cometh up from the wilderness, leaning upon her beloved? I raised thee up under the apple tree: there thy mother brought thee forth: there she brought thee forth that bare thee.
kjv.SNG_8_6: Set me as a seal upon thine heart, as a seal upon thine arm: for love is strong as death; jealousy is cruel as the grave: the coals thereof are coals of fire, which hath a most vehement flame.
kjv.SNG_8_7: Many waters cannot quench love, neither can the floods drown it: if a man would give all the substance of his house for love, it would utterly be contemned.
kjv.SNG_8_8: We have a little sister, and she hath no breasts: what shall we do for our sister in the day when she shall be spoken for?
kjv.SNG_8_9: If she be a wall, we will build upon her a palace of silver: and if she be a door, we will inclose her with boards of cedar.
kjv.SNG_8_10: I am a wall, and my breasts like towers: then was I in his eyes as one that found favour.
kjv.SNG_8_11: Solomon had a vineyard at Baalhamon; he let out the vineyard unto keepers; every one for the fruit thereof was to bring a thousand pieces of silver.
kjv.SNG_8_12: My vineyard, which is mine, is before me: thou, O Solomon, must have a thousand, and those that keep the fruit thereof two hundred.
kjv.SNG_8_13: Thou that dwellest in the gardens, the companions hearken to thy voice: cause me to hear it.
kjv.SNG_8_14: Make haste, my beloved, and be thou like to a roe or to a young hart upon the mountains of spices.

net.SNG_1_1: Solomon’s Most Excellent Love Song. The Desire for Love The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_1_2: Oh, how I wish you would kiss me passionately! For your lovemaking is more delightful than wine.
net.SNG_1_3: The fragrance of your colognes is delightful; your name is like the finest perfume. No wonder the young women adore you!
net.SNG_1_4: Draw me after you; let us hurry! May the king bring me into his bedroom chambers! The Maidens to the Lover: We will rejoice and delight in you; we will praise your love more than wine. The Beloved to Her Lover: How rightly the young women adore you! The Country Maiden and the Daughters of Jerusalem The Beloved to the Maidens:
net.SNG_1_5: I am dark but lovely, O maidens of Jerusalem, dark like the tents of Qedar, lovely like the tent curtains of Salmah.
net.SNG_1_6: Do not stare at me because I am dark, for the sun has burned my skin. My brothers were angry with me; they made me the keeper of the vineyards. Alas, my own vineyard I could not keep! The Shepherd and the Shepherdess The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_1_7: Tell me, O you whom my heart loves, where do you pasture your sheep? Where do you rest your sheep during the midday heat? Tell me lest I wander around beside the flocks of your companions! The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_1_8: If you do not know, O most beautiful of women, simply follow the tracks of my flock, and pasture your little lambs beside the tents of the shepherds. The Beautiful Mare and the Fragrant Myrrh The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_1_9: O my beloved, you are like a mare among Pharaoh’s stallions.
net.SNG_1_10: Your cheeks are beautiful with ornaments; your neck is lovely with strings of jewels.
net.SNG_1_11: We will make for you gold ornaments studded with silver. The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_1_12: While the king was at his banqueting table, my nard gave forth its fragrance.
net.SNG_1_13: My beloved is like a fragrant pouch of myrrh spending the night between my breasts.
net.SNG_1_14: My beloved is like a cluster of henna blossoms in the vineyards of En Gedi. Mutual Praise and Admiration The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_1_15: Oh, how beautiful you are, my beloved! Oh, how beautiful you are! Your eyes are like doves! The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_1_16: Oh, how handsome you are, my lover! Oh, how delightful you are! The lush foliage is our canopied bed;
net.SNG_1_17: the cedars are the beams of our bedroom chamber; the pines are the rafters of our bedroom.
net.SNG_2_1: I am a meadow flower from Sharon, a lily from the valleys. The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_2_2: Like a lily among the thorns, so is my darling among the maidens. The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_2_3: Like an apple tree among the trees of the forest, so is my beloved among the young men. I delight to sit in his shade, and his fruit is sweet to my taste. The Banquet Hall for the Lovesick The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_2_4: He brought me into the banquet hall, and he looked at me lovingly.
net.SNG_2_5: Sustain me with raisin cakes, refresh me with apples, for I am faint with love. The Double Refrain: Embracing and Adjuration
net.SNG_2_6: His left hand is under my head, and his right hand embraces me. The Beloved to the Maidens:
net.SNG_2_7: I admonish you, O maidens of Jerusalem, by the gazelles and by the young does of the open fields: Do not awaken or arouse love until it pleases! The Arrival of the Lover The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_2_8: Listen! My lover is approaching! Look! Here he comes, leaping over the mountains, bounding over the hills!
net.SNG_2_9: My lover is like a gazelle or a young stag. Look! There he stands behind our wall, gazing through the window, peering through the lattice. The Season of Love and the Song of the Turtledove The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_2_10: My lover spoke to me, saying: “Arise, my darling; My beautiful one, come away with me!
net.SNG_2_11: Look! The winter has passed, the winter rains are over and gone.
net.SNG_2_12: Blossoms have appeared in the land, the time for pruning and singing has come; the voice of the turtledove is heard in our land.
net.SNG_2_13: The fig tree has ripened its figs, the vines have blossomed and give off their fragrance. Arise, come away my darling; my beautiful one, come away with me!” The Dove in the Clefts of En Gedi The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_2_14: O my dove, in the clefts of the rock, in the hiding places of the mountain crags, let me see your face, let me hear your voice; for your voice is sweet, and your face is lovely. The Foxes in the Vineyard The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_2_15: Catch the foxes for us, the little foxes, that ruin the vineyards — for our vineyard is in bloom. Poetic Refrain: Mutual Possession The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_2_16: My lover is mine and I am his; he grazes among the lilies. The Gazelle and the Rugged Mountains The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_2_17: Until the dawn arrives and the shadows flee, turn, my beloved— be like a gazelle or a young stag on the mountain gorges.
net.SNG_3_1: All night long on my bed I longed for my lover. I longed for him but he never appeared.
net.SNG_3_2: “I will arise and look all around throughout the town, and throughout the streets and squares; I will search for my beloved.” I searched for him but I did not find him.
net.SNG_3_3: The night watchmen found me—the ones who guard the city walls. “Have you seen my beloved?”
net.SNG_3_4: Scarcely had I passed them by when I found my beloved! I held onto him tightly and would not let him go until I brought him to my mother’s house, to the bedroom chamber of the one who conceived me. The Adjuration Refrain The Beloved to the Maidens:
net.SNG_3_5: I admonish you, O maidens of Jerusalem, by the gazelles and by the young does of the open fields: “Do not awaken or arouse love until it pleases!” The Royal Wedding Procession The Speaker:
net.SNG_3_6: Who is this coming up from the wilderness like a column of smoke, like a fragrant billow of myrrh and frankincense, every kind of fragrant powder of the traveling merchants?
net.SNG_3_7: Look! It is Solomon’s portable couch! It is surrounded by sixty warriors, some of Israel’s mightiest warriors.
net.SNG_3_8: All of them are skilled with a sword, well trained in the art of warfare. Each has his sword at his side, to guard against the terrors of the night.
net.SNG_3_9: King Solomon made a sedan chair for himself of wood imported from Lebanon.
net.SNG_3_10: Its posts were made of silver; its back was made of gold. Its seat was upholstered with purple wool; its interior was inlaid with leather by the maidens of Jerusalem.
net.SNG_3_11: Come out, O maidens of Zion, and gaze upon King Solomon! He is wearing the crown with which his mother crowned him on his wedding day, on the most joyous day of his life!
net.SNG_4_1: Oh, you are beautiful, my darling! Oh, you are beautiful! Your eyes behind your veil are like doves. Your hair is like a flock of female goats descending from Mount Gilead.
net.SNG_4_2: Your teeth are like a flock of newly shorn sheep coming up from the washing place; each of them has a twin, and not one of them is missing.
net.SNG_4_3: Your lips are like a scarlet thread; your mouth is lovely. Your forehead behind your veil is like a slice of pomegranate.
net.SNG_4_4: Your neck is like the tower of David built with courses of stones; one thousand shields are hung on it— all shields of valiant warriors.
net.SNG_4_5: Your two breasts are like two fawns, twins of the gazelle grazing among the lilies.
net.SNG_4_6: Until the dawn arrives and the shadows flee, I will go up to the mountain of myrrh, and to the hill of frankincense.
net.SNG_4_7: You are altogether beautiful, my darling! There is no blemish in you! The Wedding Night: Beautiful as Lebanon
net.SNG_4_8: Come with me from Lebanon, my bride, come with me from Lebanon. Descend from the crest of Amana, from the top of Senir, the summit of Hermon, from the lions’ dens and the mountain haunts of the leopards.
net.SNG_4_9: You have stolen my heart, my sister, my bride! You have stolen my heart with one glance of your eyes, with one jewel of your necklace.
net.SNG_4_10: How delightful is your love, my sister, my bride! How much better is your love than wine; the fragrance of your perfume is better than any spice!
net.SNG_4_11: Your lips drip sweetness like the honeycomb, my bride, honey and milk are under your tongue. The fragrance of your garments is like the fragrance of Lebanon. The Wedding Night: The Delightful Garden The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_4_12: You are a locked garden, my sister, my bride; you are an enclosed spring, a sealed-up fountain.
net.SNG_4_13: Your shoots are a royal garden full of pomegranates with choice fruits: henna with nard,
net.SNG_4_14: nard and saffron, calamus and cinnamon with every kind of spice, myrrh and aloes with all the finest spices.
net.SNG_4_15: You are a garden spring, a well of fresh water flowing down from Lebanon. The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_4_16: Awake, O north wind; come, O south wind! Blow on my garden so that its fragrant spices may send out their sweet smell. May my beloved come into his garden and eat its delightful fruit!
net.SNG_5_1: I have entered my garden, O my sister, my bride; I have gathered my myrrh with my balsam spice. I have eaten my honeycomb and my honey; I have drunk my wine and my milk! The Poet to the Couple: Eat, friends, and drink! Drink freely, O lovers! The Trials of Love: The Beloved’s Dream of Losing Her Lover The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_5_2: I was asleep, but my mind was dreaming. Listen! My lover is knocking at the door! The Lover to His Beloved: “Open for me, my sister, my darling, my dove, my flawless one! My head is drenched with dew, my hair with the dampness of the night.” The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_5_3: “I have already taken off my robe—must I put it on again? I have already washed my feet—must I soil them again?”
net.SNG_5_4: My lover thrust his hand through the hole, and my feelings were stirred for him.
net.SNG_5_5: I arose to open for my beloved; my hands dripped with myrrh— my fingers flowed with myrrh on the handles of the lock.
net.SNG_5_6: I opened for my beloved, but my lover had already turned and gone away. I fell into despair when he departed. I looked for him but did not find him; I called him but he did not answer me.
net.SNG_5_7: The watchmen found me as they made their rounds in the city. They beat me, they bruised me; they took away my cloak, those watchmen on the walls! The Triumph of Love: The Beloved Praises Her Lover The Beloved to the Maidens:
net.SNG_5_8: I admonish you, O maidens of Jerusalem— If you find my beloved, what will you tell him? Tell him that I am lovesick! The Maidens to The Beloved:
net.SNG_5_9: Why is your beloved better than others, O most beautiful of women? Why is your beloved better than others, that you would admonish us in this manner? The Beloved to the Maidens:
net.SNG_5_10: My beloved is dazzling and ruddy; he stands out in comparison to all other men.
net.SNG_5_11: His head is like the purest gold. His hair is curly —black like a raven.
net.SNG_5_12: His eyes are like doves by streams of water, washed in milk, mounted like jewels.
net.SNG_5_13: His cheeks are like garden beds full of balsam trees yielding perfume. His lips are like lilies dripping with drops of myrrh.
net.SNG_5_14: His arms are like rods of gold set with chrysolite. His abdomen is like polished ivory inlaid with sapphires.
net.SNG_5_15: His legs are like pillars of marble set on bases of pure gold. His appearance is like Lebanon, choice as its cedars.
net.SNG_5_16: His mouth is very sweet; he is totally desirable. This is my beloved! This is my companion, O maidens of Jerusalem!
net.SNG_6_1: Where has your beloved gone, O most beautiful among women? Where has your beloved turned? Tell us, that we may seek him with you. The Beloved to the Maidens:
net.SNG_6_2: My beloved has gone down to his garden, to the flowerbeds of balsam spices, to graze in the gardens, and to gather lilies. Poetic Refrain: Mutual Possession The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_6_3: I am my lover’s and my lover is mine; he grazes among the lilies. The Renewal of Love The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_6_4: My darling, you are as beautiful as Tirzah, as lovely as Jerusalem, as awe-inspiring as bannered armies.
net.SNG_6_5: Turn your eyes away from me— they overwhelm me! Your hair is like a flock of goats descending from Mount Gilead.
net.SNG_6_6: Your teeth are like a flock of sheep coming up from the washing; each has its twin; not one of them is missing.
net.SNG_6_7: Like a slice of pomegranate is your forehead behind your veil.
net.SNG_6_8: There may be sixty queens, and eighty concubines, and young women without number.
net.SNG_6_9: But she is unique, my dove, my perfect one! She is the special daughter of her mother; she is the favorite of the one who bore her. The maidens saw her and complimented her; the queens and concubines praised her:
net.SNG_6_10: “Who is this who appears like the dawn? Beautiful as the moon, bright as the sun, awe-inspiring as the stars in procession?” The Return to the Vineyards The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_6_11: I went down to the orchard of walnut trees, to look for the blossoms of the valley, to see if the vines had budded or if the pomegranates were in bloom.
net.SNG_6_12: I was beside myself with joy! There please give me your myrrh, O daughter of my princely people. The Love Song and Dance The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_6_13: (7:1) Turn, turn, O Perfect One! Turn, turn, that I may stare at you! The Beloved to Her Lover: Why do you gaze upon the Perfect One like the dance of the Mahanaim?
net.SNG_7_1: (7:2) How beautiful are your sandaled feet, O nobleman’s daughter! The curves of your thighs are like jewels, the work of the hands of a master craftsman.
net.SNG_7_2: Your navel is a round mixing bowl — may it never lack mixed wine! Your belly is a mound of wheat, encircled by lilies.
net.SNG_7_3: Your two breasts are like two fawns, twins of a gazelle.
net.SNG_7_4: Your neck is like a tower made of ivory. Your eyes are the pools in Heshbon by the gate of Bath Rabbim. Your nose is like the tower of Lebanon overlooking Damascus.
net.SNG_7_5: Your head crowns you like Mount Carmel. The locks of your hair are like royal tapestries — the king is held captive in its tresses!
net.SNG_7_6: How beautiful you are! How lovely, O love, with your delights! The Palm Tree and the Palm Tree Climber The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_7_7: Your stature is like a palm tree, and your breasts are like clusters of grapes.
net.SNG_7_8: I want to climb the palm tree, and take hold of its fruit stalks. May your breasts be like the clusters of grapes, and may the fragrance of your breath be like apples!
net.SNG_7_9: May your mouth be like the best wine, flowing smoothly for my beloved, gliding gently over our lips as we sleep together. Poetic Refrain: Mutual Possession The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_7_10: I am my beloved’s, and he desires me! The Journey to the Countryside The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_7_11: Come, my beloved, let us go to the countryside; let us spend the night in the villages.
net.SNG_7_12: Let us rise early to go to the vineyards, to see if the vines have budded, to see if their blossoms have opened, if the pomegranates are in bloom— there I will give you my love.
net.SNG_7_13: The mandrakes send out their fragrance; over our door is every delicacy, both new and old, which I have stored up for you, my lover.
net.SNG_8_1: Oh, how I wish you were my little brother, nursing at my mother’s breasts; if I saw you outside, I could kiss you— surely no one would despise me!
net.SNG_8_2: I would lead you and bring you to my mother’s house, the one who taught me. I would give you spiced wine to drink, the nectar of my pomegranates. Double Refrain: Embracing and Adjuration The Beloved about Her Lover:
net.SNG_8_3: His left hand is under my head, and his right hand embraces me. The Beloved to the Maidens:
net.SNG_8_4: I admonish you, O maidens of Jerusalem: “Do not arouse or awaken love until it pleases!” The Awakening of Love The Maidens about His Beloved:
net.SNG_8_5: Who is this coming up from the wilderness, leaning on her beloved? The Beloved to Her Lover: Under the apple tree I aroused you; there your mother conceived you, there she who bore you was in labor of childbirth. The Nature of True Love The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_8_6: Set me like a cylinder seal over your heart, like a signet on your arm. For love is as strong as death, passion is as unrelenting as Sheol. Its flames burst forth, it is a blazing flame.
net.SNG_8_7: Surging waters cannot quench love; floodwaters cannot overflow it. If someone were to offer all his possessions to buy love, the offer would be utterly despised. The Brother’s Plan and the Sister’s Reward The Beloved’s Brothers:
net.SNG_8_8: We have a little sister, and as yet she has no breasts. What shall we do for our sister on the day when she is spoken for?
net.SNG_8_9: If she is a wall, we will build on her a battlement of silver; but if she is a door, we will barricade her with boards of cedar. The Beloved:
net.SNG_8_10: I was a wall, and my breasts were like fortress towers. Then I found favor in his eyes. Solomon’s Vineyard and the Beloved’s Vineyard The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_8_11: Solomon had a vineyard at Baal Hamon; he leased out the vineyard to those who maintained it. Each was to bring 1,000 shekels of silver for its fruit.
net.SNG_8_12: My vineyard, which belongs to me, is at my disposal alone. The thousand shekels belong to you, O Solomon, and 200 shekels belong to those who maintain it for its fruit. Epilogue: The Lover’s Request and His Beloved’s Invitation The Lover to His Beloved:
net.SNG_8_13: O you who stay in the gardens, my companions are listening attentively for your voice; let me be the one to hear it! The Beloved to Her Lover:
net.SNG_8_14: Make haste, my beloved! Be like a gazelle or a young stag on the mountains of spices.
